example_en: Chinese calligraphy is a beautiful art form.
example_zh: 中国书法是一种美丽的艺术形式。
phonetic_parts: c a ll i g r a ph y
collocations: Chinese calligraphy (中国书法)|modern calligraphy (现代书法)|calligraphy art (书法艺术)

example_en: We ski down the snowy hill.
example_zh: 我们从雪山上滑下来。
collocations: ski resort (滑雪胜地)|ski slope (滑雪坡)|water ski (水橇)

example_en: I wrote a simple program to draw a star.
example_zh: 我写了一个简单的程序来画星星。
collocations: computer program (计算机程序)|television program (电视节目)|exercise program (锻炼计划)

example_en: She can express her feelings with a smile.
example_zh: 她能用微笑表达自己的感受。
collocations: express oneself (表达自己)|express delivery (快 递)|express train (快车)

example_en: Our swimming instructor is very kind.
example_zh: 我们的游泳教练非常和蔼。
phonetic_parts: i n str u c t or
collocations: driving instructor (驾驶教练)|fitness instructor (健 身教练)|ski instructor (滑雪教练)

example_en: I felt scared during the thunderstorm.
example_zh: 雷雨时我感到害怕。
collocations: scared stiff (吓呆了)|scared to death (吓死了)|scared of heights (恐高)

example_en: The little boy is scared of the dark.
example_zh: 小男孩害怕黑暗。
collocations: scared (害怕的) + of (的) = 害怕的。

example_en: I fear the dark, so I sleep with a night-light.
example_zh: 我害怕黑，所以开小夜灯睡觉。
collocations: fear of heights (恐高症)|fear of public speaking (害怕公开演讲)|no fear (别怕)

example_en: It took her a long time to get over the flu.
example_zh: 她花了很长时间才从流感中恢复过来。
collocations: get over a cold (战胜感冒)|get over a breakup (走 出分手阴影)|get over the shock (从震惊中恢复)

example_en: I can eat up to three ice creams.
example_zh: 我最多能吃三个冰淇淋。
collocations: up to date (最新的)|up to you (由你决定)|up to now (到目前为止)

example_en: I wrote a short poem about spring.
example_zh: 我写了一首关于春天的小诗。
collocations: write a poem (写诗)|short poem (短诗)|love poem (情诗)

cn: 单一的，独身的；n. 单曲，单人房
example_en: I want a single scoop of ice cream.
example_zh: 我想要一勺冰淇淋。
phonetic_parts: s i n g l e
collocations: 想象你只有一个手指头(single finger)指着一样东 西。

cn: 游泳划水动作；轻抚；v. 轻抚
example_en: One strong stroke and he swam forward.
example_zh: 一个有力的划水动作，他向前游去。
collocations: stroke of luck (好运)|heat stroke (中暑)|stroke someone's ego (满足某人的自尊心)

example_en: Don't give up! I believe you can do it.
example_zh: 不要放弃！我相信你能做到的。
collocations: give up smoking (戒烟)|give up hope (放弃希望)|never give up (永不放弃)

example_en: My pen has blue ink.
example_zh: 我的钢笔里是蓝色墨水。
collocations: black ink (黑色墨水)|ink pen (钢笔)|ink cartridge (墨盒)

example_en: Please return the book to the library.
example_zh: 请把书还回图书馆。
collocations: return home (回家)|return a favor (回报恩情)|return policy (退货政策)

example_en: She helped me, and in return, I helped her.
example_zh: 她帮助了我，作为回报，我帮助了她。
collocations: in (在…里) + return (返回) = 回报。

cn: 处理；交易 / n. 交易，协议
example_en: Let's deal the cards and start the game.
example_zh: 我们发牌开始游戏吧。
collocations: deal with (处理)|big deal (了不起的事)|make a deal (达成协议)

example_en: My sister can manage her homework and piano lessons.
example_zh: 我姐姐能安排好作业和钢琴课。
phonetic_parts: m a n a g e
collocations: manage to do (设法做到)|manage risk (管理风 险)|manage expectations (管理预期)

example_en: Let's get into the car now.
example_zh: 我们现在上车吧。
collocations: get (得到) + into (进入) = 进入。

example_en: I'm not sure if I can do it, but I'll give it a go.
example_zh: 我不确定我是否能做到，但我会尝试一下。
collocations: give (给) + it (它) + a go (一次尝试) = 尝试一下。

example_en: My brother is learning kung fu.
example_zh: 我的哥哥正在学习中国功夫。
collocations: kung fu master (功夫大师)|learn kung fu (学习功 夫)|practice kung fu (练习功夫)

example_en: Push the door to go inside.
example_zh: 把门推开走进去。
collocations: push the button (按按钮)|push forward (推进)|push one's luck (冒险)

example_en: I can tie my shoes by myself.
example_zh: 我自己会系鞋带。
phonetic_parts: m y s e l f
collocations: by myself (独自)|hurt myself (伤到自己)|believe in myself (相信自己)

example_en: I eat ice cream once in a while.
example_zh: 我偶尔吃冰淇淋。
collocations: once (一次) + in a while (在一段时间里) = 偶尔。

example_en: Let’s chat about our favorite cartoons.
example_zh: 我们来聊聊最喜欢的动画片吧。
collocations: online chat (在线聊天)|live chat (实时聊天)|have a chat (闲聊)

example_en: We had a family outing to the park.
example_zh: 我们全家去公园进行了一次短途旅行。
collocations: family outing (家庭郊游)|school outing (学校郊 游)|go on an outing (去郊游)

example_en: We will go on an outing to the park next week.
example_zh: 我们下周要去公园郊游。
collocations: go (去) + on an outing (一次外出) = 去郊游。

example_en: Turn off the lights to reduce electricity use.
example_zh: 关灯可以减少用电。
collocations: reduce costs (降低成本)|reduce pollution (减少污 染)|reduce stress (减轻压力)

example_en: Too much homework gives me stress.
example_zh: 太多作业让我有压力。
collocations: job stress (工作压力)|financial stress (经济压 力)|stress management (压力管理)

example_en: Mom does yoga every morning.
example_zh: 妈妈每天早上做瑜伽。
collocations: yoga class (瑜伽课)|practice yoga (练习瑜伽)|yoga pose (瑜伽姿势)

cn: 物体；目标 v. 反对
example_en: This round object is a ball.
example_zh: 这个圆形的物体是球。
phonetic_parts: o b j e c t
collocations: object to (反对)|object lesson (实物教材)|inanimate object (无生命物体)

cn: 意大利的，意大利人的；n. 意大利人，意大利
example_en: Pizza is a famous Italian food.
example_zh: 披萨是一种著名的意大利食物。
phonetic_parts: I t a li a n
collocations: 意大利(Italy)的人就是Italian。

example_en: My dad is a computer programmer.
example_zh: 我爸爸是一名电脑程序员。
collocations: program (程序) + -er (表示人) = 程序员。

example_en: Mom allows me to play after homework.
example_zh: 妈妈允许我做完作业再玩。
collocations: allow access (允许访问)|allow time (留出时间)|allow for (考虑到)

example_en: Dogs have a good sense of smell.
example_zh: 狗的嗅觉很好。
collocations: common sense (常识)|sense of humor (幽默感)|make sense (有道理)

example_en: Getting an A is a big achievement for me.
example_zh: 得A对我来说是个大成就。
collocations: great achievement (巨大成就)|academic achievement (学业成就)|sense of achievement (成就感)

example_en: I found a shiny coin on the ground.
example_zh: 我在地上捡到一枚闪亮的硬币。
collocations: coin a phrase (创造新词)|two sides of the same coin (一体两面)|counterfeit coin (伪币)

example_en: Put a stamp on the envelope.
example_zh: 在信封上贴一张邮票。
collocations: postage stamp (邮票)|rubber stamp (橡皮图章)|stamp out (消除)

example_en: My teenage sister loves pop music.
example_zh: 我十几岁的姐姐喜欢流行音乐。
phonetic_parts: t ee n a g e
collocations: teenage years (青少年时期)|teenage pregnancy (少女怀孕)|teenage angst (青少年焦虑)

example_en: I sent a postcard from the beach.
example_zh: 我从海滩寄了一张明信片。
phonetic_parts: p o st c ar d
collocations: send a postcard (寄明信片)|vintage postcard (老 式明信片)|souvenir postcard (纪念明信片)

example_en: It's rather hot today.
example_zh: 今天相当热。
collocations: rather than (而不是)|would rather (宁愿)|rather good (相当好)

example_en: My grandpa wears old-fashioned clothes.
example_zh: 我爷爷穿老式的衣服。
collocations: old (旧的) + fashioned (时尚的) = 旧时尚的，也就 是老式的。

example_en: We learn foreign words in English class.
example_zh: 我们在英语课上学外国单词。
collocations: foreign language (外语)|foreign country (外国)|foreign policy (外交政策)

example_en: I dream of becoming a great painter.
example_zh: 我梦想成为一名伟大的画家。
collocations: dream of success (梦想成功)|dream of traveling (梦想旅行)|dream of a better future (梦想更美好的 未来)

example_en: My teacher gave me a good suggestion for reading.
example_zh: 老师给了我一个阅读的好建议。
phonetic_parts: s u gg e s ti o n
collocations: make a suggestion (提出建议)|accept a suggestion (接受建议)|follow a suggestion (遵循 建议)

example_en: Every failure is a step to success.
example_zh: 每一次失败都是通向成功的一步。
collocations: complete failure (彻底失败)|business failure (商业 失败)|fear of failure (害怕失败)

example_en: My teacher’s words inspire me to study hard.
example_zh: 老师的话鼓舞我努力学习。
collocations: inspire confidence (激发信心)|inspire creativity (激发创造力)|inspire hope (激发希望)

example_en: Our teacher is strict about homework.
example_zh: 我们的老师对作业要求严格。
collocations: strict rules (严格的规定)|strict parents (严格的父 母)|strict diet (严格的饮食)

example_en: Surprisingly, the small boy won the race.
example_zh: 出乎意料地，小男孩赢得了比赛。
phonetic_parts: s ur pr i s ing l y
collocations: surprisingly good (出奇的好)|surprisingly effective (出奇的有效)|surprisingly little (出奇的 少)

example_en: So far, I have finished two books.
example_zh: 到目前为止，我已经读完两本书了。
collocations: so (如此) + far (远) = 像走了很远，到现在为止。

example_en: I sing on the school stage.
example_zh: 我在学校舞台上唱歌。
collocations: on stage (在舞台上)|stage fright (怯场)|set the stage (准备就绪)

example_en: Most importantly, wash your hands before eating.
example_zh: 最重要的是，吃饭前要洗手。
phonetic_parts: i m p or t a nt l y
collocations: more importantly (更重要的是)|most importantly (最重要的是)|importantly, consider (重要的是，考 虑)
